title: Last Call: Who Killed the Singer?

roles.label: Bartender
roles.what_i_know: I heard Nico pushing people all night. He was trying to make somebody crack.
roles.what_im_hiding: I comped drinks I should not have comped.

motive: Nico was about to expose reimbursement fraud and side-money theft
Nico found internal corruption and was going to expose it publicly
Nico was using private secrets, humiliation, and emotional blackmail to control someone
Nico ruined someone's chance to win the karaoke finals
Nico publicly embarrassed the wrong person and got killed for revenge
Nico stumbled into a random backstage confrontation
method: Backstage confrontation with a weighted stage tool, then staged as an accident
Confrontation in a restricted area, followed by a redirected cover story
Private confrontation that turned violent, then staged to blend into bar chaos
A public fight that escalated in front of witnesses
A random drunken fall behind the stage
evidence: The financial trail combined with Nico being lured backstage over a fake mic problem
The internal records motive plus the fact that the first story protected management most
The secret meeting plus proof that the real motive was private control, not bar politics
The public argument at the bar
The back hall route by itself

version_a.label: Version A
version_a.motive: Nico was about to expose reimbursement fraud and side-money theft
version_a.method: Backstage confrontation with a weighted stage tool, then staged as an accident
version_a.strongest_evidence: The financial trail combined with Nico being lured backstage over a fake mic problem
version_a.reveal_copy: Tonight’s killer was the Sound Tech. Nico found a money trail that never should have existed — fake reimbursements, side deals, and enough proof to ruin her. He thought he could corner her after finals. Instead, she pulled him backstage with a fake mic problem, confronted him in the hall, and killed him with a weighted stage tool. Then she let the room believe it was chaos, bad luck, and one too many grudges colliding at once.

version_b.label: Version B
version_b.motive: Nico found internal corruption and was going to expose it publicly
version_b.method: Confrontation in a restricted area, followed by a redirected cover story
version_b.strongest_evidence: The internal records motive plus the fact that the first story protected management most
version_b.reveal_copy: Tonight’s killer was the Manager. Nico got too close to what was really happening inside this bar — comp abuse, payroll manipulation, hidden financial rot. He thought he could drag it into the light during finals. Vivian knew exactly what would happen if he did. She confronted him, killed him, and then used the confusion of the room to point suspicion anywhere but upward.

version_c.label: Version C
version_c.motive: Nico was using private secrets, humiliation, and emotional blackmail to control someone
version_c.method: Private confrontation that turned violent, then staged to blend into bar chaos
version_c.strongest_evidence: The secret meeting plus proof that the real motive was private control, not bar politics
version_c.reveal_copy: Tonight’s killer was the Ex-Lover. Nico had been using private secrets like weapons. He humiliated, blackmailed, and controlled the people closest to him because he thought they would never push back. Tonight he pushed too far. The meeting was private, the confrontation was real, and what happened next was not an accident.

version_d.label: Version D
version_d.motive: Nico publicly embarrassed the wrong person and got killed for revenge
version_d.strongest_evidence: The public argument at the bar
version_d.reveal_copy: Tonight’s killer was the Bartender. Nico humiliated the wrong person in a room full of witnesses, then kept pressing after he should have backed off. Jax knew exactly what Nico was drinking, exactly when he would go back for another round, and exactly how to make the death look like one more piece of chaos from finals night. The loudest clue was right in front of everyone — the argument at the bar that turned personal.

version_e.label: Version E
version_e.motive: Nico stumbled into a random backstage confrontation
version_e.method: A random drunken fall behind the stage
version_e.strongest_evidence: The back hall route by itself
version_e.reveal_copy: Tonight’s killer was Security. This was the messiest truth of the night: Nico walked into the wrong space at the wrong time and pushed the wrong person. Omar was already on edge, already covering things he didn’t want anyone else seeing, and the confrontation in the hall went bad fast. What looked like a drunken fall was a cover born out of panic. The route itself told the story — Nico died where only one person was supposed to control access.

version_f.label: Version F
version_f.motive: Nico ruined someone's chance to win the karaoke finals
version_f.method: A public fight that escalated in front of witnesses
version_f.reveal_copy: Tonight’s killer was the Karaoke Host. Finals night was supposed to belong to Lena — the room, the pacing, the show itself. Nico blew that up. He meddled with the running order, turned the crowd, and threatened to ruin the night on purpose. The confrontation wasn’t hidden for long. It started out in the open, carried into the chaos, and by the time the room realized what had happened, Lena had already buried the truth under the noise of her own event. The deleted story post was the tell: she knew exactly when the night turned and why.

0.content: I built this finals night. Nico treated it like something he could wreck just to prove a point.

Whatever was happening between Nico and the host felt bigger than flirting or stage nerves.
If finals collapsed tonight, the host had more to lose than anybody.
